items.title: India’s app market is booming — but global platforms are capturing most of the gains
items.summary: Non-gaming apps, led by streaming and AI, are driving growth, even as India's spending per user lags global peers.

items.title: Shade lands $14M to let creative teams search their video libraries in plain English
items.summary: Shade has its own filesystem that lets you "stream" files directly to your local drive

items.title: Tesla just increased its spending plan to $25B — here’s where the money is going
items.summary: Tesla's planned capex for 2026 is three times higher than what the company has historically spent. Its CFO said, as a result, Tesla will have a negative free cash flow the rest of the year.

items.title: Google updates Workspace to make AI your new office intern
items.summary: Google has introduced a host of new automated functions into Workspace, all of which are driven by Workspace Intelligence, its new AI system.

items.title: Elon Musk admits millions of Tesla owners need upgrades for true ‘Full Self-Driving’
items.summary: The admission could open Tesla to legal challenges after it spent years promising customers they were just one software update away from owning fully autonomous cars.

items.title: Hands on with X’s new AI-powered custom feeds
items.summary: X's AI-powered custom timelines are replacing Communities, with Grok-curated feeds...and new ad slots.

items.title: LinkedIn’s CEO is moving on; please hold your tearful video tributes
items.summary: Ryan Roslansky has stepped down as LinkedIn's CEO after six years running the world's largest professional network. Dan Shapero, the company's COO, takes over immediately.

items.title: Tesla Q1 revenue rises, driven by EV sales and FSD subscriptions
items.summary: Sales rebounded a bit from the first quarter of 2025, as Tesla throws a ton of money at massive bets like robotics, AI, and its own chip fab.

items.title: NASA’s Artemis II moon mission shows space-to-Earth laser comms can scale
items.summary: Observable Space and Quantum Opus teamed up to capture data beamed back from space.

items.title: How SpaceX preempted a $2B fundraise with a $60B buyout offer
items.summary: Cursor was on track to close a $2 billion funding round this week but chose to halt discussions after SpaceX offered a $10 billion "collaboration fee" and a path to a $60 billion acquisition.

items.title: France confirms data breach at government agency that manages citizens’ IDs
items.summary: The French government agency that issues and manages national IDs, passports, and other documents announced that hackers stole the personal information of an unspecified number of citizens.

items.title: Apple fixes bug that cops used to extract deleted chat messages from iPhones
items.summary: The iPhone and iPad bug allowed law enforcement using forensic tools to read messages that had long been deleted by the Signal app.

items.title: From the stage to the future: Where are Startup Battlefield’s alumni now?
items.summary: We wanted to show you what happens after the confetti falls. We checked in with some of our recent alumni, many of whom have sat down with us on Build Mode: The Founder Survival Guide, TechCrunch's podcast for founders at every stage.

items.title: Google Cloud launches two new AI chips to compete with Nvidia
items.summary: Google's newest TPUs are faster and cheaper than the previous versions. But the company is still embracing Nvidia in its cloud — for now.

items.title: Google turns Chrome into an AI co-worker for the workplace
items.summary: Google brings Gemini-powered "auto browse" capabilities to Chrome for enterprise users, letting workers automate tasks like research, data entry, and more.

items.title: Fusion power may not be sci-fi. Just ask the people who sunk $5B into it.
items.summary: Fusion energy has been “20 years away” for decades, but has the science finally caught up? Private investment in fusion companies surged from $10 billion to $15 billion in just months, and the money is coming from places you wouldn’t expect.  Watch as Rebecca Bellan and guest host Tim De Chant sit down with Rachel Slaybaugh, general partner at DCVC on this episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast. The trio breaks […]

items.title: Threads is adding Live Chats to boost real-time engagement
items.summary: Users can send messages, photos, videos, links, and emoji reactions in Live Chats.

items.title: Google makes an interesting choice with its new agent-building tool for enterprises
items.summary: Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform takes an interesting approach: It is geared for IT and technical users.

items.title: AI Overviews are coming to your Gmail at work
items.summary: The AI Overviews will offer instant summaries pulled from across multiple emails.

items.title: Rivian R2 production has started despite tornado damage to factory
items.summary: The company's all-important mass-market EV is just about ready to start heading to customers.
